mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal consumption expenditures (proxied by RSAFS) signal strong aggregate demand, which drives corporate earnings growth and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC is negative over any 2-year rolling window, or RSAFS month-over-month positive changes lead to negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When the civilian unemployment rate falls below 4.5%, the labor market is overheated, putting upward pressure on wages and inflation, which prompts monetary tightening and depresses equity prices over the following month.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 4.5%, forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or average excess return over baseline is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) and stagnant federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signal eroding real yields and potential for future tightening, which is negative for equity valuations over the next quarter.
regime_hint: low and stable policy rate environment
failure_condition: In periods of rising CPI and steady FEDFUNDS, 60-day forward returns are positive more than 60% of the time, or the combined effect does not have incremental predictive power over single series.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) serve as a leading indicator of construction activity and consumer demand. In a low policy rate regime (FEDFUNDS below 1%), rising housing starts signal robust economic momentum with limited tightening risk, boosting equity prices.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 1%, HOUST month-over-month increases do not predict positive 20-day SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.10 over 5-year rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) exceeding 200,000 signals strong labor demand and income growth, which drives consumer spending and boosts equity prices over the following trading week.
failure_condition: When PAYEMS increases by more than 200k, the next 5-day SPX return is negative in more than 50% of cases, or the average excess return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) combined with rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicates synchronized expansion in both manufacturing and consumption, reducing default risks and raising expected corporate cash flows, leading to positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: When both INDPRO and RSAFS rise month-over-month, 5-day SPX returns are negative more frequently than 45% of the time, or the joint signal does not outperform individual signals in a logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth above 3%), the central bank is forced to raise interest rates eventually, and any further increase in inflation accelerates that timeline, depressing equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL YoY is above 3%, month-over-month acceleration does not predict negative 60-day SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.05 over 3-year rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: A lower federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically raising present values. Conversely, higher rates compress valuations. This relationship is always active and predicts a negative correlation between FEDFUNDS level and forward 5-day returns.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return Rank IC with FEDFUNDS is positive over any 2-year window, or the average return when FEDFUNDS is above its 1-year median is higher than when below (p > 0.05).
